title: Brown Contracting v. City Renovations — quantum meruit for accepted work
relief_sought: Reasonable value of $96,400 in materials and labor furnished
key_facts: Plaintiff completed framing, drywall, and finish carpentry on defendant's downtown renovation between May and September 2025.
Parties never executed a written subcontract although a draft circulated by email referenced a $96,400 lump sum.
Defendant's project manager signed daily inspection logs accepting the work without objection.
Defendant invoiced the building owner for plaintiff's scope and was paid in full but refused to remit to plaintiff.
